items.title: From Simulations to Stars: Computational Astrophysics
items.description: An interactive talk on computational methods in astrophysics. Demonstrated fluid simulations and discussed how numerical methods help us understand cosmic phenomena like interstellar turbulence and starburst regions.

items.title: Magnetic Fields & Turbulence: Why Stars Form the Way They Do
items.description: A 15-minute explainer video breaking down complex astrophysics concepts. Explores the role of $$ \mathbf{B} $$-fields in turbulent media and star formation efficiency.

items.title: Python for Computational Astronomy
items.description: A hands-on workshop teaching practical coding skills for astronomers. Covered numerical simulations, data analysis, and visualization techniques using NumPy and Matplotlib.
